mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts signals a strengthening demand for housing, which in turn drives economic growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods of increasing housing starts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A crossing of the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A joint increase in payroll employment and a decrease in the unemployment rate signals a strengthening labor market, which in turn drives economic growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods of joint labor market improvement are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production signals a strengthening supply chain, which in turn drives economic growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods of increasing industrial production are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A crossing of the GDP above 3% annualized growth rate signals a strengthening demand for goods and services, which in turn drives economic growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in periods of high GDP growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A joint decrease in the federal funds rate and an increase in the consumer price index signals a loosening monetary cycle that increases inflation expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods of joint monetary loosening and inflation increase are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decrease in the unemployment rate below 5% signals a tightening labor market, which in turn drives economic growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods of low unemployment are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A joint increase in housing starts and a decrease in the unemployment rate signals a strengthening demand for housing and labor, which in turn drives economic growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods of joint housing and labor market improvement are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
